name: stray-research
description: Repo-local Codex plugin for research, analysis, product direction, and terms checks.

interface.shortDescription: Research and analysis plugin for current, source-backed investigation and decision support.
interface.longDescription: A repo-local Codex plugin for source-backed research workflows. It separates concise web briefs, bounded domain research, and long-form multi-track reports; distills supplied pages safely; explores ideas and makes evidence-backed product decisions; designs MCP servers; summarizes Japan news; retrieves JMA weather and identified-company gBizINFO data; triages GitHub maintenance read-only; conducts global or Japan-focused public patent research; checks practical API or SaaS terms; and analyzes Japanese horse racing with current-source, confidentiality, prompt-injection, legal-boundary, and responsible-use guardrails.
defaultPrompt: Research a current topic on the web and return a concise sourced brief.
Plan and conduct deep research, optionally splitting independent tracks across subagents, then return a documented report with sources, caveats, and uncertainty.
Extract the useful content from a web page and turn it into clean markdown.
Investigate a specialized technical, regulatory, market, or academic topic and return a concise sourced brief.
Generate research-backed ideas or option sets using current web signals before choosing a direction.
Design a product direction or feature brief using current web research.
Plan or review an MCP server before implementation.
Summarize the latest Japanese news in a fixed brief format using current web sources.
Retrieve Japan Meteorological Agency station, AMeDAS, recent historical, time-series, and forecast weather data with exact timestamps and caveats.
Search and summarize Japanese corporate information from gBizINFO with corporate numbers, endpoint provenance, and missing-data boundaries.
Triage GitHub maintenance work and recommend the next actions.
Plan or conduct a public-web global patent research workflow with cautious, source-backed findings.
Conduct a Japan-focused public patent search with J-PlatPat, Japanese terminology, FI, F-term, a reproducible search log, and no legal conclusions.
Check the current terms or usage restrictions for an API or SaaS before integrating or shipping it.
Analyze a Japanese horse racing race using official, licensed, or user-provided data, with probability ranges, value checks, risk notes, and no automated wagering.
